topic: From idea to bank-ready in one sitting — covering all 10 DBO / Riyada programs in English or Arabic
format: List the 9 documents a complete feasibility study includes — and what each one tells the bank
post:
A bank-ready feasibility study has 9 parts. Most applicants submit 3.

Here's what the reviewer actually needs to see:

1. **Executive Summary** — decides whether they keep reading
2. **Project Description** — what you're building and who's running it
3. **Market Analysis** — proof that demand exists before you spend a rial
4. **Technical Plan** — equipment, space, licenses. All of it.
5. **Organisational Structure** — the right people in the right roles
6. **Financial Projections** — 3–5 year forecast, not a rough guess
7. **Funding Requirements** — exact amount, exact use
8. **Risk Assessment** — banks actually respect honesty here
9. **Implementation Timeline** — a realistic start-to-launch schedule

Leave out any one of these and your application stops at the first desk. Not because your idea is bad. Because the file is incomplete.

Most rejections come down to that. Not a weak business concept — just a study that doesn't match what the reviewer is looking for.

DBO and Riyada run 10 different programs. One of them almost certainly fits what you're planning. The gap is usually just knowing how to write for that audience.

Build a bank-ready feasibility study free in ~10 minutes — covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility
